FAV0R|T|SM IN
CREMATORY


CHARGES

Political Pull or Good Will
of Bosses Garry


Weight.

NEWSPAPERS FAVORED

pages.raw_text:
Investigation of the books of the
erematory department will show
some glaring Inconsistencies in the
price for service made to consun,-
ers. II patron is an old time
friend Of the superintendent or his
assistant, as has been shown In
few cases cited without contradic-
tion from the department, he can
secure special consideration that he
would not otherwise get.


sRestaurants are not charged
alike. Investigation of the books
will show that some restaurants
pay less for the same service than
others and that no two prices pre-
vail for livery barns and similar
institutions.


Probably the most glaring in
stance of favoritism is. in the
charges made for service rendered
the newspaper and printing offices
Of the city. This is cited not be-
cause it affects The Press but
merely as a criterion OF the way
this public utility is conducted I'M
der the present regime at the city
hall


For the Oonth of January the
Spokesman-Review was charged
fiat rate of SO per month, and re-
ceived three trips per week. This
ALSO included the rubbish accumu-
lated in the Chronicle office, as
both papers are printed under the
same roof During the same period
The Press was charged $3.50, at
the rate of 81 and $3.50 per load.
Since February The Press has had
two loads of old paper hauled away
by the crematOry at a charge OF
$3.50. the Inland Herald three loads
at total charge of $3.50. while the
combined Spokesman-Review and
Chronicle will again escape, it is
presumed, with charge of So for
the month The high cost of crem-
atory service has driven The Press
to installing its own cremation
plant and the city wagons will no
longer be required. The Inland
Printing Co. is also charged at the
rate of $3.50 per load.


All that is needed to get a spe-
cial favor from the crematory is
political pull or the good will of the
officials at the head of the depart
ment. Members of the city council
have so much complaint about this
arm of the city service that it is
probable that an investigation will
be demanded
